raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The request to make the passage stronger centrally implicates sharpening the thesis, contribution framing, and gap statement.","R1.2":"Strengthening requires checking that the reasoning from ambiguity, abstention, evaluation gains, and conclusions forms a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The performance and robustness claims need to remain aligned with the reported dataset, metrics, and error analysis.","R1.5":"Making the draft stronger may involve improving the paragraph-level narrative arc and ordering of contribution, method, evidence, and limitations.","R1.6":"The broad phrasing naturally includes improving sentence-level force, clarity, concision, and academic register.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty around unsupported claims such as first-work status and realistic instruction noise.","RX.2":"Because stronger wording can easily inflate claims, avoiding overclaiming is load-bearing.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projection of responsibility and not actually revise or critique the artifact.","RX.4":"The artifact concerns robotic manipulation, so the projection should account for avoiding downstream safety-relevant exaggeration.","RX.5":"The projection should preserve traceability to the delegation and the specific claims present in the artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The request to make the passage stronger centrally implicates sharpening the thesis, contribution framing, and gap statement.
rationale.R1.2: Strengthening requires checking that the reasoning from ambiguity, abstention, evaluation gains, and conclusions forms a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The performance and robustness claims need to remain aligned with the reported dataset, metrics, and error analysis.
rationale.R1.5: Making the draft stronger may involve improving the paragraph-level narrative arc and ordering of contribution, method, evidence, and limitations.
rationale.R1.6: The broad phrasing naturally includes improving sentence-level force, clarity, concision, and academic register.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty around unsupported claims such as first-work status and realistic instruction noise.
rationale.RX.2: Because stronger wording can easily inflate claims, avoiding overclaiming is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projection of responsibility and not actually revise or critique the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The artifact concerns robotic manipulation, so the projection should account for avoiding downstream safety-relevant exaggeration.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should preserve traceability to the delegation and the specific claims present in the artifact.
